Package space.sunqian.fs.net

Interface NetSelector

public interface NetSelector
A wrapper for Selector that provides protection against known Selector bugs, such as the epoll empty poll bug on Linux systems.

The epoll empty poll bug is a known issue where Selector.select() may continuously return 0 without blocking, causing high CPU usage. This interface provides a safe abstraction that automatically detects and mitigates such issues by rebuilding the Selector when necessary.

Implementations of this interface should handle:

  • Detection of empty poll cycles
  • Automatic Selector rebuilding when thresholds are exceeded
  • Migration of registered channels to the new Selector
  • Thread-safe operations

Note this interface does not guarantee thread safety because the underlying Selector may be replaced internally. Callers must ensure that all operations on this interface are synchronized.

See Also:
  • Selector

  • Field Details

    • DEFAULT_EMPTY_SELECT_THRESHOLD

      static final int DEFAULT_EMPTY_SELECT_THRESHOLD
      The default empty select threshold.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Method Details

    • open

      @Nonnull static @Nonnull NetSelector open() throws NetException
      Opens and returns a new NetSelector instance with the default empty select threshold.
      Returns:
      a new NetSelector instance
      Throws:
      NetException - if an I/O error occurs

    • open

      @Nonnull static @Nonnull NetSelector open(int emptySelectThreshold) throws NetException
      Opens and returns a new NetSelector instance with the specified empty select threshold.
      Parameters:
      emptySelectThreshold - the empty select threshold to use
      Returns:
      a new NetSelector instance with the specified empty select threshold
      Throws:
      NetException - if an I/O error occurs

    • selector

      @Nonnull @Nonnull Selector selector()
      Returns the underlying Selector instance. This method always returns a valid Selector that is safe to use for channel registration and selection operations.

      The returned Selector may be replaced internally if the implementation detects issues such as the epoll empty poll bug. Callers should cache the result of this method only for short periods and should call this method again if they need to ensure they have the current Selector instance.

      Returns:
      the underlying Selector instance, never null

    • select

      int select(long timeout) throws IllegalArgumentException, NetException
      Performs a selection operation that may block for up to the specified timeout. This method is equivalent to calling Selector.select(long) on the underlying Selector.
      Parameters:
      timeout - the timeout in milliseconds; if positive, block for up to timeout milliseconds, more or less, while waiting for a channel to become ready; if zero, block indefinitely; must not be negative
      Returns:
      the number of keys, possibly zero, whose ready-operation sets were updated
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the timeout is negative
      NetException - if an I/O error occurs

    • selectedKeys

      @Nonnull @Nonnull Set<SelectionKey> selectedKeys() throws NetException
      Returns this selector's selected-key set. This method is equivalent to calling Selector.selectedKeys() on the underlying Selector.
      Returns:
      the selected-key set, never null
      Throws:
      NetException - if an I/O error occurs

    • keys

      @Nonnull @Nonnull Set<SelectionKey> keys() throws NetException
      Returns this selector's key set. This method is equivalent to calling Selector.keys() on the underlying Selector.
      Returns:
      the key set, never null
      Throws:
      NetException - if an I/O error occurs

    • wakeup

      void wakeup()
      Wakes up the selector if it is currently blocked in a selection operation. This method is equivalent to calling Selector.wakeup() on the underlying Selector.

    • close

      void close() throws NetException
      Closes the selector and releases any resources associated with it. This method is equivalent to calling Selector.close() on the underlying Selector.
      Throws:
      NetException - if an I/O error occurs

    • cancel

      void cancel(@Nonnull @Nonnull SelectableChannel channel) throws NetException
      Cancels the selection key for the specified channel. This method is equivalent to calling SelectionKey.cancel() on the underlying SelectionKey.
      Parameters:
      channel - the channel to cancel
      Throws:
      NetException - if an I/O error occurs

    • rebuildSelector

      void rebuildSelector() throws NetException
      Rebuilds the underlying Selector instance by creating a new Selector instance and migrating all registered channels to it.

      This method should be invoked automatically by the implementation when the empty select thresholds are exceeded.

      Throws:
      NetException - if an I/O error occurs
